qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated arbitrary JavaScript code execution via node-custom-function endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class and root cause. The finding describes RCE via /api/v1/node-custom-function endpoint, while the advisory describes sensitive data leak via /api/v1/public-chatbotConfig/:id endpoint. Different vulnerable functions (executeCustomFunction vs getSinglePublicChatbotConfig), different endpoints, different impact type (RCE vs information disclosure).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a sensitive data leak in /api/v1/public-chatbotConfig/:id endpoint where getSinglePublicChatbotConfig returns full flowData without authorization. The finding describes a different vulnerability: RCE via /api/v1/node-custom-function endpoint. The finding does not identify the vulnerable chatbotConfig endpoint, the missing authorization check, or the credential exposure root cause described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory specifies vulnerable function: getSinglePublicChatbotConfig in packages/server/src/services/chatflows/index.ts
Advisory impact: Exposure of API keys, HTTP authorization headers, and internal configuration from password fields
Finding vulnerable function: executeCustomFunction (different from advisory)
Finding impact: RCE via arbitrary JavaScript execution (different vulnerability class)
